title: Level 203
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:The Day I Died}} {{Blurbg|file=Void-1317028375.gif}} {{CSS| content=.page__main {background-color: rgba(var(--theme-page-background-color--rgb), 0.00); }} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Stylish&display=swap'); }}

The First Day
{{Underline|323232}} {{Underline|191919}} Bodies dancing in hanging air. The empty promises of the nullscape still wish to tie them up, dancing them like puppets on string. A process no one expects to experienced, being tugged around by nothingness, delicately sat within what was and what could never be. A tipping point between the light and the dark. Was this a life worth living? With the new knowledge of the afterlife, should the bounds of morality and goodness be questioned? Perhaps there would have been a better time to think through this. If only the mind would silence its mania. The remote in the hands of others as life itself whips by faster and faster. It's afraid. The brain doesn't know death, nor should it ever. The brain is scarred. It goes beyond mind and soul, it's the last stand to try and save its fleshy host. Soon the thoughts will stop, but that's a problem for the next day. For now, try to ignore the body's performance to an audience of nothingness. Nothing else will come from thinking for now. Thoughts mean nothing right now. Perhaps fall asleep again. Maybe this "death" was a creation of a tired and scared mind. It has braved quite the horrors, it was sure to suffer the consequences from the misery eventually. The brain needs a break, try to give it one.




The Second Day
{{Underline|323232}} {{Underline|191919}} They move too fast to notice. Every repeat the brain moves faster, checking again and again. Soon all that remains are continuous blobs of colors. Slowly they mold together, the chaos eventually calms. The brain doesn't know how death works. So does nothing else. Eventually new solutions will be born from the mental recesses. This can't be all that death is. And so the mind manufactures its expectations. The separation of mind and body has never felt so potent. A weight of familiar tolerance, shed to explore this final resting place. The body quits its dancing too, a sad pile of meat and bones falls to the floor. Such rejection felt natural, as if freed from a prison unknown to all. Without such shackles, the mind is not contained to the body. Without space, its consumed by emptiness. A playground to wonder about what awaited it. Front row tickets to all of this, the soul could only follow along, left to bear the emotions that the mind wants to leave behind. What is it to say no? Such a long unity, this could not be how they part ways, right? There would simply be no value to such. Without one, the other has no purpose. It's symbiotic. Right?




The Third Day
{{Underline|323232}} {{Underline|191919}} "Are we sure this is the right move?" Soul followed behind Mind, their descent into this artificial labyrinth has been hosted purely by Mind. Mind didn't look back to Soul, not even for a moment, to focused on the path. Every turn it made the world around it agreed and followed its demands. Soul could only voice its complaints as the journey continued. It seemed to stretch on for hours until Mind stopped, holding Soul behind it. Well, holding in the metaphysical sense of a provided barrier only produced because they both agreed it would be there. Normal bounds didn't exist here, it was only the rules they agreed upon in that moment. "I think I found the right way." Mind spoke up, willfully ignoring any other comments Soul made during the expedition. Mind saw the winding halls of intricacy and symbols, guiding it to a goal only it seemed to be able to see. Soul's only ability was whining anyway, it didn't benefit it to tell it about what Mind saw. "We made it to one of the trials." Mind's eye buried itself within Soul, now able to see this trial. A scale of an older god. One who's fall from grace mirrored the empire it was founded in. One side a feather, and the other emptiness. The feather sat above what wasn't there. Perhaps this was an impossible feat. How does a feather weight less than nothing? Though as Soul thought over this, it seemed its answer materialized before it, a collective of feathers. Not one separated itself from the others. "I'd hate to brag, but I already solved it." Soul walked to the scales with pride, brushing past Mind as it was left silent. Afraid? Maybe. Was it ever so slightly hopeful that Soul's guess was wrong, not very slightly. And Soul was, the feathers weighed more than the singular one. But as Soul watched the scales turn, it wasn't quick to blame itself. "Stop hoping. Stop thinking." It snapped at Mind. The two sat in silence for a few moments, before the singular feather started to gain weight. Slowly it overpowered the group of feathers. "Look at that." Soul spoke matter of factly, shifting their attention back to Mind. "Dumb luck." Mind asserted, walking past Soul. It avoided looking at the scale puzzle, instead continuing to the grand open doors that came from the solution. With no other path, Soul followed Mind. They stepped out into a cerulean sky, the sun casting a powerful beam onto the land. They witness mind and soul return to one, taking along with their mortal form to forever spend their afterlife continuing what they loved. At least that's what it seemed like to Soul. Soul stepped ahead of Mind, but was stopped rather quickly. "C'mon, let's go back. I don't like this one." "You've been here for half a second." "First off, it's been a minute, not half a second. Second off, I know the mortal best, this is not the afterlife that we wanted. So if you want to find an idyllic resting place, you're going to need to follow me."




The Fourth Day
{{Underline|323232}} {{Underline|191919}} For a moment mind and soul return to body. Looking back within itself to decide where best to continue their journey. The mind can concoct powerful images, and map out a world that the brain can process. But the Soul makes the unrealized realized, the painter of dreams and daydreaming. A vessel of emotional exploration which the mind sees better without. The mind is not perfect though, as a perfect mind can see the benefit of the soul. The mind will refuse though, certain a reality based in rationality is the only reality worth living. Engaging in only what is obtainable and logical. A refusal to the power of thought that it is provided with. It desires only the facts. But facts are bland and simple, non beneficial to the nature of reality. The nature of reality, a place in which the soul thrives. The soul know the power of the mind and sees itself important too. For the soul needs the other to prevent overwhelming power. No soul is useless however, as it's a tool to develop relationships and processing. Yet it is wished to be controlled by the mind. A puppet on a string. The dancing body in the empty space. A performance for no one, a show for nothing. Convulsing flesh will lose its essence again, as they will continue searching for an end. What is the purpose of dying?




The Fifth Day
{{Underline|323232}} {{Underline|191919}} Traveling was a guarantee for them both, each of them continuing on a supposedly predetermined path, one created by Mind to find an end which was perfect. With the occasional minds eye Soul was provided, it could see to the split roads that Mind randomly picked. Left three times, right once, left one time, center thrice. A random array of options seemingly infinitely long. "Do you have any clue where we are going?" A silent whine emanated from it, and Mind didn't seem to care too much about its concerns. "Of course I do, we made it here." Mind stepped out to a open space, with tiny dots in the voidal sky connecting, disconnecting, and connecting elsewhere. There was no pattern, no form to be made of certain principles. Yet their movement was certain and provided no room for uncertainty. It was connected to another structure, or it was not. As Soul stared at the arrangement, a wind of words whisped by its ears. In an infinite arrangement of atoms and values, are we ever the same ship? If our body can form to something new and changes every moment, are we ever the same person? What makes you you and not the you that you once were. If it was a change of atoms you'd never be you again. The words blended into a fine slurry for Soul, as it stood still to decipher the meaning. However Mind didn't seem so confused by the prompt. "Of course not. Bodies change all the time. They are different than what they once were." Of course Mind saw it as such, it had no tie to the physical. It is further separated from the brain, unable to feel the laughter and rosy cheeks of a pleasant day, or the salty tears of sobbing. And it seemed the atoms disagreed too, as nothing changed. "Are you sure that's your answer? Apparently it doesn't think so." "Yes, of course I do." "The believe it to be, however that works." Mind's thoughts wandered, connecting answers to agreements, and as they seemed to form, so did the dots. Bundling together to create a doorway, and in their eyes they could see another life being born. "What happens if we go through there?" "I'm pretty sure you'd lose everything and be made totally anew. I don't think it's a good move." Mind was quick to leave Soul, but as it was the only way out of the labyrinth, Soul could only follow. The door closed as it turned its back, and atoms returned to their clashing and leaving. It seemed nothing satisfied Mind, and it was bound to drive Soul mad.




The Sixth Day
{{Underline|323232}} {{Underline|191919}} "Can you stop following me? I'll return to you when I find the perfect afterlife." "You wouldn't come back for me." Soul protested, continuing on Mind's heels as they walked through further nothingness. "And you need me here. We both need to find a resting place." Mind shook its head as Soul said "we". Despite it being noticed, Soul didn't push the argument further. Time and time again the game had to be played. And the game would come quickly, as two angels stood before them, each guarding a door. "Oh, it's this puzzle..." Mind spoke quietly to itself, trying to remember the proper answer to its own challenge. Soul sighed and stepped forward, looking towards one of the angels. It's eyes bore down on Soul, a flame of power and righteous duty. "Open your door" The angel obliged, opening the door up to a fiery inferno, a realm of suffering and agony clear by the screams of those who died a normal death. They had no choice but to be condemned. Soul and Mind didn't. Mind seemed displeased with Soul's actions, and Soul asked before Mind could ask anything. "Why do you keep attempting these strange puzzles? First it was the scales, then it was a reincarnation game, and now were talking to angels to go to heaven and hell. What's the deal with all this?" "Because the afterlife simply can't be as simple as this. You were tricked to believe that it'd all be fine and dandy. Probably we'd need to prove ourselves if we died. That's how it always was." Life is a collection of challenges after all, death couldn't be so easy. "We have no idea what the afterlife is like Mind. Nobody does." Soul trudged back to the other essence, taking Mind away from heaven's gates. "And do not start with wanting to go to heaven. You'd give up and try to go elsewhere within 5 minutes. Maybe our body is still safe, we can figure this out later." "... fine. But only this time."




The Seventh Day
{{Underline|323232}} {{Underline|191919}} '''Level 203''', also known as '''The Day I Died''' is an eternal realm of nothingness on initial viewings. However, this level can adjust its environment upon mental processing. Level 203 forms around the mind of any wanderer, resulting in a unique sight for each individual. While each version is unique to the wanderer, there are common happenings that make each round through Level 203 similar. One such case is the afterlife. All abstractions of the afterlife resemble those created in various religions. These often also contain a sort of challenge or puzzle to prove they are worthy of entering this realm. Yet, the puzzle is only solved by the wanderer's mind believing it to be, there are no actions that can be taken out of thought which would lead to a success. These puzzles can also manifest as popular thought experiments or game theory, often tying back to the religious practice. Regardless the solution remains the same, to think of the puzzle as being completed rather than directly acting on it. The only way into Level 203 is to die via being consumed by the nothingness. Presumably this is the result of the inability to correctly process nothingness, though death by nothingness does not guarantee entrance to Level 203. And because of such, the only way to exit Level 203 is to wake up. Wake up. {{Collapse |initial=Wake up. |final=Wake up. |body=
